metadata.districtModel: five sections; five district slots per section; D13 is the seat of power

cityDNA.coreFantasy: A small rodent-like beastfolk city where Rodentfolk Burrow-Market of the Lantern Roots, Terrace Granary Beacon, Moon Phase Windows Sovereign Registry Court, and the five-section district spine turn local identity into functional civic space.
cityDNA.readerTakeaway: Nettleburrow should feel immediately identifiable through exact landmarks and materials, not through generic fantasy-city shorthand.
cityDNA.civilizationThesis: Nettleburrow organizes power through Moon Phase Windows Sovereign Registry Court, public response through Terrace Granary Beacon, civic memory through Rodentfolk Burrow-Market of the Lantern Roots, and daily life through S1 Agricultural Terraces Civic Hall, S2 Underworld / Hidden Economy Civic Hall, S3 Civic Services Hall, S4 Slums / Informal Settlement Civic Hall, S5 Low Residential Civic Hall.
cityDNA.visualIdentity: Rootwood, Fieldstone, Copper, Willow, Limewash, Subterranean, Undercity Foundation, Limestone Caverns, Beast Bonding, Ancestor Guidance, Hearth Blessing, and exact anchors including Moon Phase Windows Sovereign Registry Court, Terrace Granary Beacon, Rodentfolk Burrow-Market of the Lantern Roots, Tenement Court, Black Market Arcade, Scrap Market, Agricultural Terraces Civic Hall, Hanging Gardens Monument, Seed Hall Nook, Underworld / Hidden Economy Civic Hall.
cityDNA.culturalIdentity: A small rodent-like beastfolk civic culture where architecture, public obligations, roads, landmark approaches, and everyday work must all read as part of the same civilization.
cityDNA.symbolicIdentity: The city symbol system is built from exact names and visible functions: Rodentfolk Burrow-Market of the Lantern Roots, Terrace Granary Beacon, Moon Phase Windows Sovereign Registry Court, and section anchors such as Agricultural Terraces Civic Hall, Underworld / Hidden Economy Civic Hall, Civic Services Hall, Slums / Informal Settlement Civic Hall, Low Residential Civic Hall.

canonLocks: Nettleburrow uses city code NET.
The Wonder is Rodentfolk Burrow-Market of the Lantern Roots.
The Beacon is Terrace Granary Beacon.
The Seat of Power is Moon Phase Windows Sovereign Registry Court and occupies District 13.
The city uses five sections with five tactical district slots per section.

The source-backed section spine is: S1 Agricultural Terraces Civic Hall, S2 Underworld / Hidden Economy Civic Hall, S3 Civic Services Hall, S4 Slums / Informal Settlement Civic Hall, S5 Low Residential Civic Hall.

rejectedReads: Do not treat Nettleburrow as a generic fantasy settlement with renamed landmarks.
Do not collapse the five-section board into a single mood or single district.
Do not replace exact anchor names with generic summaries.
Do not invent missing street-section landmarks when a street source is absent.
cautionFlags: Keep every named Wonder, Beacon, Seat, section civic hall, major landmark, monument, and minor nook exact when present in sidecars.
If an image appears to deviate from a sidecar, preserve the exact name but avoid overclaiming visual details not supported by the source package.
Every district asset should remain usable for narrative scenes, tactical board dioramas, and city map backgrounds.

environment.environmentalLogic: The environment should make Nettleburrow's exact civic anchors plausible at street scale: terrain routes people toward section landmarks, lighting clarifies public authority, and water or landscape features remain source-backed rather than decorative filler.

streets.appearance: Streets should show Nettleburrow's exact anchor system at pedestrian scale: S1 Agricultural Terraces Civic Hall, S2 Underworld / Hidden Economy Civic Hall, S3 Civic Services Hall, S4 Slums / Informal Settlement Civic Hall, S5 Low Residential Civic Hall. streets.movement: Movement should be readable for tactical-board assets: citizens pass between 25 district slots, section anchors, D13, service streets, and landmark approaches without visual clutter hiding the civic purpose.
sounds: landmark-specific sounds from water, bells, animals, machines, or crowds when source-backed
smells: worn stone, timber, metal, plaster, or fabric
streets.lighting: Use the package's source-backed civic lighting, daylight/golden-hour reads, lanterns, and beacon glow. Lighting must help identify authority, safety, and landmark function.
streets.weatherEffects: Weather should reinforce the local terrain and materials without overwhelming the city identity or inventing unsupported climate extremes.
streets.dayCycle: Day scenes emphasize errands, trade, records, repairs, rituals, food, and section-level movement.
streets.nightCycle: Night scenes emphasize lantern routes, closed thresholds, guarded landmarks, quiet work, and beacon visibility.

housing.wealthy: Prestige housing clusters near Moon Phase Windows Sovereign Registry Court, high terraces, or landmark-facing routes, using the best source-backed materials and controlled views of civic space.
housing.middle: Shop-over-house units, courtyard homes, guild-adjacent apartments, and family rooms near section anchors.
housing.poor: Smaller back-street rooms, work-adjacent housing, shared courtyards, service alleys, patched materials, and dense but still city-specific lived-in detail.
